definitions: go was built to solve real frustrations at google… slow build times, complex codebases, and difficulty writing concurrent software
the goal was a language that's simple to write, fast to compile, and efficient to run

simple syntax: go has a minimal, clean syntax inspired by C but without much of the complexity; there are very few keywords, and the language deliberately omits features like inheritance and operator overloading
compiled & fast: go compiles directly to machine code, making programs fast like C/C++, but compiles much faster than those languages
statically typed: types are checked at compile time, catching errors early but Go's type inference (x := 5) makes it feel less verbose than Java
garbage collected: memory is managed automatically, so you don't manually allocate/free memory like in C

built-in concurrency: this is go's standout feature. it has goroutines (lightweight threads) and channels (for communication between them), making concurrent programming much easier than in most languages
strong standard library: go ships with excellent built-in packages for HTTP servers, JSON, file I/O, testing, and more often without needing third-party libraries

types.name: backend web servers & APIs
types.description: fast, efficient servers and REST APIs

types.name: cloud infrastructure tools
types.description: docker, kubernetes, and terraform are all written in Go

types.name: microservices
types.description: small, independent services that work together
